description: Key proof techniques, barriers, and methods for attacking unsolved problems.

facts.context: Diagonalization arguments (Cantor, Turing) prove impossibility results by:
facts.truth: constructing objects that differ from every element in a countable list at that element's index

facts.context: Relativization barrier for P vs NP shows that:
facts.truth: there exist oracles A and B where P^A = NP^A and P^B ≠ NP^B
distractors: P = NP relative to all oracles

facts.context: The algebrization barrier (Aaronson-Wigderson 2009) extends relativization showing:
facts.truth: techniques that algebrize cannot separate P from NP
distractors: algebraic methods solve P vs NP

facts.context: Geometric Complexity Theory (GCT) approaches P vs NP using:
facts.truth: algebraic geometry and representation theory to prove circuit lower bounds
distractors: has already separated P from NP

facts.context: Cohen's forcing technique in set theory was used to prove:
facts.truth: independence of Continuum Hypothesis from ZFC (neither provable nor refutable)

facts.context: The compactness theorem in model theory states:
facts.truth: a theory has a model iff every finite subset has a model

facts.context: The Hardy-Littlewood circle method is used in additive number theory by:
facts.truth: analyzing generating functions via complex contour integrals, separating major and minor arcs

facts.context: Modern sieve methods in number theory provide:
facts.truth: upper bounds on primes in sets but cannot prove infinitude (parity barrier)

facts.context: Ricci flow, used by Perelman to prove Poincaré, evolves metric by:
facts.truth: ∂g/∂t = -2Ric(g), deforming geometry to find canonical structures

facts.context: Modular forms were essential to Wiles' proof of Fermat's Last Theorem via:
facts.truth: the modularity theorem linking elliptic curves to modular forms

facts.context: Paris-Harrington theorem shows that certain combinatorial statements:
facts.truth: are true but unprovable in Peano arithmetic (first natural independence result)

facts.context: Reverse mathematics classifies theorems by the axioms needed to prove them. Most analysis theorems need:
facts.truth: WKL₀ or ACA₀ (weak systems much weaker than full ZFC)
